THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUED THE MATTER TO JAN-09-2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 ON AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: JANET POND, CSR #5292

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO 1/26/12 AT 9:30 A.M. FOR FINAL HEARING. FURTHER PAPERS BY RYDER DUE 1/17/12; REPLY BY HYLEN DUE 1/20/12. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077
POS OF DECLARATION OF WILLIAM MITCHELL RYDER; RESPONSE OF WILLIAM MITCHELL RYDER FILED BY DEFENDANT RYDER, WILLIAM MITCHELL

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF RICHARD HYLEN'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 2, 2012 TO ENABLE MR. RYDER TO RESPOND TO THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY MR. HYLEN ON JANUARY 20 AND JANUARY 24. THE EVIDENTIARY RECORD IS NOW CLOSED AND THUS, ABSENT COURT ORDER, NO ONE SHALL SUBMIT ANY FURTHER EVIDENCE ON THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION MOTION. MR. RYDER MAY FILE SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION PAPERS NO LATER THAN JANUARY 27 LIMITED TO THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY MR. HYLEN ON JANUARY 20 AND JANUARY 24. COURTESY COPIES OF THE PAPERS FILED BY MR. HYLEN ON JANUARY 20 AND ANY FURTHER SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION PAPERS THAT MAY BE FILED BY MR. RYDER MUST BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 302 BY 4PM ON JANUARY 30, 2012. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LA VENA WARD, CSR # 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RICHARD HYLEN'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - MR. HYDEN IS THE PERSON RESPONSIBLE WITH THE PAYROLL PROVIDER; NO MONEY IS TO BE PAID TO EITHER PARTY UNLESS THERE IS ENOUGH MONEY FOR LIQUID ASSETS AT THAT TIME; THAT MONEY WOULD BE PAID TO COVER THE PAYROLL OF EMPLOYEES OTHER THAN MR. HYDEN AND MR. RYDER AND AT LEAST A $5,000.00 CUSHION AND ANYTHING OVER THAT THEY CAN SPLIT NOT TO EXEED $7,240.00. THE PARTIES STIPULATED THAT THEY WILL NOT CHARGE ANY PERSONAL EXPENSES TO THE SATEL AMERICAN EXPRESS OR OTHER ACCOUNT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: MATTER ARGUED. AT CONCLUSION OF HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY DEFENDANT WILLIAM MITCHELL RYDER IS NOT IN FURTHER CONTEMPT OF THE COURT'S FEB-02-2012 AND APR-30-2012 ORDERS, THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. A FURTHER HEARING TO DETERMINE IF THE DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO AN ATTORNEY FOR CONTEMPT PROCEEDINGS IS SET FOR OCTOBER 5, 2012 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. PARTIES ARE ORDERED PRESENT IN COURT ON OCTOBER 5, 2012. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY DEFENDANT IS NOT IN CONTEMPT OF THE COURT'S FEB-2-2012 ORDER ON PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT IS FOUND TO BE IN CONTEMPT AND IS TO PAY PLAINTIFF ATTORNEY FEES OF $4,000.00. THE REST OF THE ATTORNEY FEES CAN BE SOUGHT AT THE END OF THE CASE, IF APPROPRIATE. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO FINE DEFENDANT IS DENIED. THE COURT ORDERS THAT DEFENDANT NOT WRITE CHECKS FOR HIMSELF OR FOR PERSONAL EXPENSES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

Off Calendar Apr-30-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY DEFENDANT IS NOT IN CONTEMPT OF THE COURT'S FEB-2-2012 ORDER ON PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.
Appearances: William Ryder (no card provided) defendant and responding party appearing in propria persona; Unidentified Counsel for plaintiff (no card provided).
Argued and the court ruled as follows: defendant is found to be in contempt and is to pay plaintiff attorney fees of $4,000.00. The rest of the attorney fees can be sought at the end of the case, if appropriate. Plaintiff's request to fine defendant is denied. The court orders that defendant not write checks for himself or for personal expenses. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288

Plaintiff Richard Hylen'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TODD ROBERTS, TEL. NO. (650) 364-8200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
RICHARD HYLEN, PLAINTIFF WAS ALSO PRESENT IN COURT;
ATTY. SEAN E. PONIST OF PETERSON, MARTIN & REYNOLDS, TEL. NO. 399-2900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT; WILLIAM RYDER, DEFENDANT WAS ALSO PRESENT IN COURT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff Richard Hylen's Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction - MR. HYDEN IS THE PERSON RESPONSIBLE WITH THE PAYROLL PROVIDER; NO MONEY IS TO BE PAID TO EITHER PARTY UNLESS THERE IS ENOUGH MONEY FOR LIQUID ASSETS AT THAT TIME; THAT MONEY WOULD BE PAID TO COVER THE PAYROLL OF EMPLOYEES OTHER THAN MR. HYDEN AND MR. RYDER AND AT LEAST A $5,000.00 CUSHION AND ANYTHING OVER THAT THEY CAN SPLIT NOT TO EXEED $7,240.00. THE PARTIES STIPULATED THAT THEY WILL NOT CHARGE ANY PERSONAL EXPENSES TO THE SATEL AMERICAN EXPRESS OR OTHER ACCOUNT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
Plaintiff Richard Hylen'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction is
Continued to February 2, 2012 to enable Mr. Ryder to respond to the evidence submitted by Mr. Hylen on January 20 and January 24. The evidentiary record is now closed and thus, absent court order, no one shall submit any further evidence on the preliminary injunction motion. Mr. Ryder may file supplemental opposition papers no later than January 27 limited to the evidence submitted by Mr. Hylen on January 20 and January 24. Courtesy copies of the papers filed by Mr. Hylen on January 20 and any further supplemental opposition papers that may be filed by Mr. Ryder must be delivered to department 302 by 4pm on January 30, 2012.
